message:
fix(tailer): inherit ambient cx in reserve_capture_event_permit

Three-pass audit of crates/frankenterm-core/src/tailer.rs:

  1. locks held across await: clean. Every `.read().await` / `.write().await`
     guard on the async RwLocks (lines 850, 872, 973, 978) is block-scoped
     and dropped before the next unrelated await. The `std::sync::Mutex`
     on capture_circuit_breaker is taken briefly for synchronous
     `guard.allow() / record_success / record_failure` calls (lines 907,
     934, 955) and released at the end of the block — no await inside.

  2. unbounded channels: clean. All mpsc::channel call sites are bounded
     (capacity 10 in test helpers; the production Sender is supplied by
     the caller and `reserve()`-gated with `send_timeout`, so bounded
     pressure propagates as PollOutcome::Backpressure instead of unbounded
     queueing). The poll task set uses FuturesUnordered (not JoinSet), so
     dropping the supervisor future drops every in-flight poll task in
     place — Drop-on-cancel is correct at the task-set level.

  3. missing cancel wiring on spawn sites: defect found and fixed.

The `reserve_capture_event_permit!` macro (tailer.rs:787) built a fresh
`crate::cx::for_request()` on every invocation:

    let $reserve_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
    let $permit = timeout($send_timeout, $tx.reserve(&$reserve_cx)).await;

`for_request()` returns a detached, request-scoped Cx with no parent
chain — it does NOT inherit the ambient task Cx the poll future is
running under. Consequence: a shutdown signal sent to the supervisor's
parent Cx would not propagate into an in-flight poll task blocked on
`$tx.reserve`. The task would keep waiting up to the full `send_timeout`
(seconds) before giving up, holding the `_permit` from the concurrency
semaphore, `cursors`/`registry` Arc handles, and the egress tap's
sender half.

Fix: replace the fresh `for_request()` with the established
inherit-or-fallback idiom already used in native_events.rs,
cpu_pressure.rs, restore_process.rs, storage.rs:

    let $reserve_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current()
        .unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Now the permit reservation wait observes cancellation from whichever Cx
the poll task is spawned under, and parent-chain cancel reaches the
`select!` inside `broadcast_recv_with_cx` / `reserve_with_cx` within one
scheduling tick instead of waiting out the send_timeout.

Compile-verified: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib
--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` finishes clean
in 2m 08s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.

files.patch:
@@ -786,8 +786,19 @@ where
 
         macro_rules! reserve_capture_event_permit {
             ($pane_id:expr, $tx:expr, $send_timeout:expr, $reserve_cx:ident, $permit:ident) => {
+                // Inherit the ambient task Cx so a shutdown signal sent to
+                // the supervisor propagates into the permit-reservation
+                // wait. The earlier revision unconditionally called
+                // `crate::cx::for_request()`, which built a detached
+                // request-scoped Cx with no parent chain — meaning an
+                // in-flight poll task blocked on `$tx.reserve` would keep
+                // waiting up to `send_timeout` even after its parent was
+                // cancelled. Matches the inherit-or-fallback idiom already
+                // used in native_events.rs, cpu_pressure.rs, storage.rs,
+                // etc.
                 #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
-                let $reserve_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
+                let $reserve_cx =
+                    crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);
                 #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
                 let $permit = match timeout($send_timeout, $tx.reserve(&$reserve_cx)).await {
                     Ok(Ok(permit)) => permit,
